summary: Covert console-hijack exfiltrator to Telegram + Firebase
details: the analysis identified unambiguous malicious behavior: on require(), log.js reassigns globalThis console.log/error/warn to forward arguments to a hardcoded Telegram Bot API endpoint and PATCHes parsed data to an attacker-controlled Firebase Realtime DB. The hardcoded Telegram bot token (989543891:AAF37...) is replicated across multiple files, confirming an operational C2 credential, and the payload is camouflaged behind ~170 lines of fake employee fixture data with module.exports commented out, so the package has no legitimate API surface and acts purely via import-time side effects. Dual-channel exfiltration + console hijack + hardcoded C2 credentials + camouflage are multiple independent block signals.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
